calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Section 17200 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Roger Mead for petitioner Prakash H. Mehta (via Zoom).
David Monsour for respondent Asha Mehta (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Instructions Pursuant to Probate Code Section 17200, et seq.

The Court questioned whether San Francisco County is the proper venue. Mr. Mead argued that venue was proper because the prior trustee resided and died in San Francisco, the successor trustee retained San Francisco counsel, and all day-to-day trust activities had been conducted through counsel in San Francisco. He further stated that no party had objected to venue and that San Francisco was the only logical forum for administration.

Mr. Mansour stated he did not object to venue in San Francisco.

The Court noted that the Notice of Trust Administration identified Arlington, Virginia as the principal place of administration pursuant to Probate Code Section 16016.7, and that Probate Code Section 17005(c) requires petitions concerning a trust to be filed in the county of its principal place of administration.

The Court dismissed the petition for lack of venue. The Court stated that if petitioner believes the notice was erroneous, he may file a new petition correcting the venue designation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Section 17200 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Continued To Oct-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
